objective: Stop tool-curious, consideration-stage pitchers/parents mid-scroll with a 'what does the product actually show me' hook, then convert to a Pitch Nav analysis. Shares its scenes 2-7 verbatim with the other launch ads — only the hook (scene 1) and framing change.
audience: Pitchers/parents who are comparing training tools and have not committed yet — consideration-stage, ideal for retargeting.
hook: A pitching video goes in. This is what comes back.
coreInsight: A delivery can look clean at full speed while a specific mechanical pattern — the upper half rotating before the front side is stable — is easy to miss without frame-by-frame video, and Pitch Nav surfaces it automatically.
openLoop: The hook promises to show exactly what the product outputs — the viewer keeps watching to see the actual result.
payoff: The freeze frame and blue overlay show the exact moment and pattern the product would flag; the report screen proves it.

scenes.cameraDirection: Real full-speed side-view pitch, mid-windup, hard cut in — no logo, no intro.
scenes.soundDesign: Single low tick on the cut in.

assetReferences.description: Full-speed side-view pitch, subject A, rep 1

scenes.cameraDirection: Same rep continues, ease into slow motion at foot strike.
scenes.voiceOver: At full speed, everything here looks normal.

scenes.cameraDirection: Freeze frame at foot strike; blue joint-marker overlay animates onto shoulders/hips.
scenes.voiceOver: Stop it right at foot strike, and the upper half is already rotating before the front side is stable.
scenes.soundDesign: Freeze-frame stop accent; overlay draw-on sound, subtle.

assetReferences.notes: Motion-graphic overlay only — pitcher footage underneath is real.

scenes.cameraDirection: Split screen: rep 1 freeze frame (left) and rep 2 freeze frame (right), same phase, same overlay.
scenes.voiceOver: Same pattern. Another pitch. Pitch Nav caught it automatically.

assetReferences.description: Full-speed + slow-motion side-view pitch, subject A, rep 2

scenes.cameraDirection: Cut to a single drill rep addressing front-side stability; caption states the priority.
scenes.voiceOver: One priority: stabilize the front side before the rotation starts. That's what the drill work targets.
bRoll: Drill demonstration, front-side stability drill, real footage
scenes.soundDesign: Music bed enters, low under VO.

scenes.cameraDirection: Screen recording: report view scrolling to priority + drills + progress calendar.
scenes.voiceOver: Pitch Nav turns one slow-motion video into a clear breakdown — one priority, personalized drills, and progress you can track.

scenes.cameraDirection: Static end card: wordmark small at top, CTA centered, price beneath.
scenes.voiceOver: Start your Pitch Nav analysis. $25 a month.

voiceOverScript: At full speed, everything here looks normal. Stop it right at foot strike, and the upper half is already rotating before the front side is stable. Same pattern. Another pitch. Pitch Nav caught it automatically. One priority: stabilize the front side before the rotation starts. That's what the drill work targets. Pitch Nav turns one slow-motion video into a clear breakdown — one priority, personalized drills, and progress you can track. Start your Pitch Nav analysis. $25 a month.
bRollInstructions: Two reps from the same athlete, matched phase-to-phase. Drill footage is real, one rep, no more. Product screens are real, live UI, no mockups.
editingNotes: Frame changes every 1-3 seconds throughout, per content/video-templates/timelines/30s.md. Open on the pitch, not the logo. Hold the freeze frame only as long as the overlay is animating. See content/editing-style-rules.md before final cut.
soundDesign: Music bed under everything, ducked below voice-over. One whoosh on the speed ramp, one tick on the hook cut, one soft accent on the overlay draw-on. No glitch transitions.

assetReferences.description: Subject A, two reps, full-speed + slow-motion, side view
Report/dashboard walkthrough: priority, drills, progress calendar
Low-key, tension-building sports-tech bed, no lyrics
